attacking_style: Initiates attacks on penultimate climbs to establish winning moves. Prefers to launch offensive moves earlier in mountain stages rather than waiting for final climb, forcing rivals to respond.
team_tactics: Surprisingly limited domestique support even when in race lead. Required to chase personally and perform defensive work rather than being protected, suggesting weak team positioning or leadership prioritization conflicts.
terrain_preference: Multi-climb mountain stages where attacking on earlier climbs can establish separation. Strong on sustained climbs where he can apply consistent pressure on marked rivals.
vulnerability: Exposed when forced into defensive/chasing roles for teammates despite being strongest rider. Workload management issues when required to police moves personally. Team obligations override individual strength.
key_patterns: Attacks penultimate climbs to initiate winning moves, not waiting for finale (2 observations)
Performs own chase work even as race leader, lacking team protection (2 observations)
Team tactical conflicts compromise his GC position despite superior form (2 observations)
Strong enough to be marked by entire GC group but undermined by domestique duties (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: Despite appearing strongest all week at Tour de Suisse, team obligations and forced chasing cost him time and energy, suggesting mismanagement of clear winning form
